name: jsonpath-ng (Python JSONPath Implementation)

performance.notes: PLY-based parser; fast for typical query sizes

agent_readiness.notes: JSONPath query language for Python dicts. from jsonpath_ng import parse. expr = parse('$.store.book[*].author'). matches = expr.find(data). [match.value for match in matches]. Update: expr.update(data, 'new_value'). DatumsPath: match.full_path. Recursive descent: $..author. Filter: $.book[?(@.price < 10)]. Index: $.book[-1]. Slice: $.book[0:2]. Alternatives: jmespath (Amazon, simpler), glom (more Pythonic).

gotchas: PLY parser warning printed on import (annoying in production)
Filter syntax is non-standard across JSONPath implementations
Multiple Python JSONPath libraries with incompatible syntax
best_when: JSONPath queries against nested Python dicts/JSON
Update operations on specific JSON paths
When JSONPath is the required query language (e.g., JSON Schema validators)
avoid_when: Complex Pythonic data extraction (use glom or toolz)
summary: jsonpath-ng implements JSONPath for querying nested Python dicts. parse('$.store.book[*].author').find(data) returns matches. Recursive descent: $..author. Filters: $.book[?(@.price < 10)]. Update: expr.update(data, value). Apache-2.0. No type annotations, PLY parser warning on import. For simpler JSON queries use jmespath; for Python-native extraction use glom.
